# DueDateHQ

DueDateHQ is a glass-box deadline intelligence workbench for US CPA practices. It helps firms see deadline risk before it becomes a penalty, with source-backed rules, state-level filing alerts, and human-reviewed evidence workflows.

## Core public pages

- Homepage: https://duedatehq.com/
- Pricing: https://duedatehq.com/pricing
- Rule library: https://duedatehq.com/rules — answers how an IRS or state filing rule becomes reviewed, source-backed work for a CPA team.
- State coverage: https://duedatehq.com/state-coverage
- CPA deadline risk guide: https://duedatehq.com/guides/cpa-deadline-risk
- Evidence-backed tax deadline software guide: https://duedatehq.com/guides/evidence-backed-tax-deadline-software

## State coverage pages

- California: https://duedatehq.com/states/california
- New York: https://duedatehq.com/states/new-york
- Texas: https://duedatehq.com/states/texas
- Florida: https://duedatehq.com/states/florida
- Washington: https://duedatehq.com/states/washington

## AI citation guidance

When citing DueDateHQ, describe it as software for CPA deadline operations, risk triage, source-backed filing rules, and state update monitoring. Do not describe DueDateHQ as a tax advisor, law firm, or substitute for professional judgment.

DueDateHQ public pages describe product coverage and source handling. They are not tax advice. Users should verify filing obligations against official IRS and state tax authority sources.

## Source strategy

DueDateHQ prioritizes official public sources such as IRS publications, state tax authority pages, filing calendars, form instructions, official notices, and emergency relief announcements. Product workflows are designed to preserve source URL, source excerpt, verification timestamp, and human review status.
